brief.topic: Stephen F. Austin was called the Father of Texas because he helped 300 families settle in Texas in the 1820s — Austin, the state capital, is named after him

passageRow.subject: social-studies
passageRow.title: Stephen F. Austin: Father of Texas
passageRow.body:
## Stephen F. Austin: Father of Texas

Stephen F. Austin is known as the Father of Texas. He helped many families move to Texas. In the 1820s, he brought 300 families to settle in Texas. These families started new lives and built homes. They worked together to create a community.

Austin was a leader. He helped the families find good land to farm. He also made sure they had what they needed. Because of his hard work, many people wanted to come to Texas. The capital city of Texas, Austin, is named after him. Today, people remember Stephen F. Austin for his important role in Texas history.

passageRow.topicNotes: Stephen F. Austin's contribution to settling Texas and the naming of Austin.

brief.topic: Sam Houston was a general and the first president of the Republic of Texas — he led Texas to freedom from Mexico, and Houston, the largest city in Texas, is named after him

passageRow.subject: social-studies
passageRow.title: Sam Houston: Texas Leader
passageRow.body:
## Sam Houston: Texas Leader

Sam Houston was a very important person in Texas history. He was a general who helped lead Texas to freedom from Mexico. People called him a hero because he worked hard to make Texas a safe and happy place for families.

After Texas became free, Sam Houston became the first president of the Republic of Texas. This was a big job, and he worked hard to help Texas grow. He wanted to make sure that everyone in Texas had a good life. Sam Houston was a leader who cared about people and wanted them to live in peace.

The largest city in Texas is named after Sam Houston. It is called Houston! Many people live there today. Sam Houston's work and leadership are remembered by Texans every day. He helped shape the Texas we know now.

passageRow.topicNotes: Sam Houston as a key figure in Texas history, focusing on his leadership and legacy.

brief.topic: the six flags that have flown over Texas (Spain, France, Mexico, Republic of Texas, Confederate States, United States) and why Texas has had so many — different countries ruled at different times

passageRow.subject: social-studies
passageRow.title: The Six Flags of Texas
passageRow.body:
## The Six Flags of Texas

Texas is a special place. It has had six different flags. Each flag stands for a country that once ruled Texas. These countries are Spain, France, Mexico, the Republic of Texas, the Confederate States, and the United States.

First, Spain ruled Texas. They came to explore and set up missions. Then, France had a short time in Texas. They wanted to trade and explore. After that, Mexico ruled Texas. Many families moved to Texas during this time.

Later, Texas became its own country. This was called the Republic of Texas. Texas even had its own president! After a while, Texas joined the United States. For a short time, Texas was part of the Confederate States. But soon after, it became part of the United States again. Each flag tells a story about the history of Texas.

passageRow.topicNotes: Simplified history of the six flags over Texas.

brief.topic: three major regions of Texas a second-grader can name — the Coastal Plains in the east, the Hill Country in the center, the Mountains and Basins in the west — and one fact about each

passageRow.subject: social-studies
passageRow.title: Three Major Regions of Texas
passageRow.body:
## Three Major Regions of Texas

Texas is a big state with many different places. We can learn about three major regions: the Coastal Plains, the Hill Country, and the Mountains and Basins.

The Coastal Plains are in the east. This area is near the Gulf of Mexico. It is flat and has many trees. The weather is often warm and wet. Many people live here because of the cities and the ocean.

The Hill Country is in the center of Texas. This region has many hills and rocky land. It is a great place for hiking and seeing wildflowers in the spring. The Hill Country is famous for its beautiful views and clear rivers.

The Mountains and Basins are in the west. This region is dry and has tall mountains. The highest mountain in Texas, Guadalupe Peak, is here. The land is more open, and fewer people live in this area.

Each region in Texas is special. From the ocean in the Coastal Plains to the hills in the Hill Country and the mountains in the west, Texas has many different places to explore.

brief.topic: three Texas rivers — the Rio Grande (border with Mexico), the Brazos (longest river inside Texas), and the Colorado (runs through Austin) — and why rivers matter for cities and farms

passageRow.subject: social-studies
passageRow.title: Texas Rivers
passageRow.body:
## Texas Rivers

Texas has many important rivers. Three big ones are the Rio Grande, the Brazos, and the Colorado. The Rio Grande makes a long border between Texas and Mexico. It helps people know where Texas ends and Mexico begins. The Brazos River is the longest river inside Texas. It flows through many cities and towns. The Colorado River runs through Austin, the capital of Texas.

Rivers are very important for cities and farms. They give water to people and animals. Cities use river water for drinking and washing. Farms need river water to grow plants and feed animals. Rivers can also be fun! People like to swim, fish, and play near rivers. Rivers help keep Texas a great place to live.

Each river has its own story and helps in a special way. The Rio Grande is famous for its long path. The Brazos River connects many parts of Texas. The Colorado River brings water to Austin. Rivers are like friends to the land, giving it life and beauty.

brief.topic: goods are things you can hold (apples, books, toys) and services are jobs people do for others (cutting hair, fixing cars, teaching) — telling them apart with examples

passageRow.subject: social-studies
passageRow.title: Goods and Services
passageRow.body:
## Goods and Services

Goods are things you can touch and hold. They are items you buy at a store or market. For example, apples are goods. You can eat them. Books are goods too. You can read them. Toys are also goods. You can play with them. Goods are everywhere around us!

Services are different from goods. Services are jobs people do to help others. You cannot hold a service in your hand. For example, a hairdresser gives a service by cutting hair. A mechanic provides a service by fixing cars. Teachers offer a service by helping students learn. Services are important because they help us in many ways.

It is fun to find goods and services in your community. Next time you are at a store, look around. See if you can spot the goods. Then, think about the services you use every day. Maybe you visit a doctor for a check-up. That is a service. Goods and services work together to make our lives better!
passageRow.topic: goods are things you can hold (apples, books, toys) and services are jobs people do for others (cutting hair, fixing cars, teaching) — telling them apart with examples
passageRow.topicNotes: Explains difference between goods and services with examples.

brief.topic: why we have laws in Texas — to keep people safe, to settle problems fairly, and to protect things — three example laws (stop at red lights, no littering, school is for kids age 6+)

passageRow.subject: social-studies
passageRow.title: Why We Have Laws in Texas
passageRow.body:
## Why We Have Laws in Texas

Laws help keep us safe and happy. In Texas, we have rules called laws. Laws tell us what we can and cannot do. They help people live together nicely. They also help protect things we care about, like our parks and schools.

One law says we must stop at red lights. This law keeps cars and people safe. When everyone follows this rule, it helps prevent accidents. Another law says we cannot litter. Littering means throwing trash on the ground. This law helps keep Texas clean. It protects our parks and keeps our neighborhoods nice.

There is also a law about school. In Texas, kids must start school when they are six years old. School helps kids learn and grow. It is important for everyone to get an education. These laws help us live in a safe and fair community. Laws make Texas a great place to live!

brief.topic: why cowboys were so important in early Texas — they took care of cattle, drove herds north on long trails, and the cattle business made Texas grow

passageRow.subject: social-studies
passageRow.title: Cowboys in Early Texas
passageRow.body:
## Cowboys in Early Texas

Cowboys were very important in early Texas. They took care of the cattle. Cattle are big animals that give us beef. Cowboys would ride horses to watch over the cattle. They made sure the cattle were safe and healthy.

Cowboys also drove herds north on long trails. A herd is a big group of cattle. The trails were paths that led to other places. Cowboys would guide the herd to towns where people wanted to buy the cattle. This was a big job because the trails were long and dusty.

The cattle business helped Texas grow. Selling cattle made money for Texas. This money helped build new towns and roads. Cowboys worked hard to help Texas become a busy place. They are still remembered today for their important work.
